meaning: adj.粗糙的,荒芜的,苛刻的,刺耳的,刺目的
examples: His voice was harsh and unmusical.
If that sounds harsh, it is meant to.
The minister received some harsh criticism.
Nine years seems rather a harsh penalty for a crime that did not itself harm anyone.
Predictably, this was what caused the harsh words in the Delhi talks this week.
